instruction:
Let
$\xi$: the boundary point between light rain and moderate rain,
$\eta$: the boundary point between moderate rain and heavy rain, with the respective distributions:
$$
\xi \! : \! N(1, 4), \qquad \eta \! : \! N(2, 4).
$$
Try using the trisection method to determine the membership functions of the three fuzzy concepts: light rain, moderate rain, and heavy rain.

Options:
A. $$
A_{1}\left(x\right)=1-\phi\left(\frac{x-2}{2}\right), \\A_{2}\left(x\right)=\phi\left(\frac{x-1}{2}\right)
\\A_{3}\left(x\right)=1-A_{1}\left(x\right)-A_{2}\left(x\right)
\\\phi(x)=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2\pi}}\int_{-2x}^{x}\mathrm{e}^{-\frac{t^{2}}{2}}\mathrm{d}t
$$
B. $$
A_{1}\left(x\right)=\phi\left(\frac{x-1}{2}\right), \\A_{2}\left(x\right)=\phi\left(\frac{x-2}{3}\right)
\\A_{3}\left(x\right)=1-A_{1}\left(x\right)-A_{2}\left(x\right)
\\\phi(x)=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2\pi}}\int_{-\infty}^{x}\mathrm{e}^{-t^{2}}\mathrm{d}t
$$
C. $$
A_{1}\left(x\right)=1-\phi\left(\frac{x-1}{2}\right), \\A_{2}\left(x\right)=\phi\left(\frac{x-2}{2}\right)
\\A_{3}\left(x\right)=1-A_{1}\left(x\right)-A_{2}\left(x\right)
\\\phi(x)=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2\pi}}\int_{-\infty}^{x}\mathrm{e}^{-\frac{t^{2}}{2}}\mathrm{d}t
$$
D. $$
A_{1}\left(x\right)=1-\phi\left(\frac{x-1}{3}\right), \\A_{2}\left(x\right)=\phi\left(\frac{x-2}{4}\right)
\\A_{3}\left(x\right)=1-A_{1}\left(x\right)-A_{2}\left(x\right)
\\\phi(x)=\frac{1}{\sqrt{\pi}}\int_{-\infty}^{x}\mathrm{e}^{-\frac{t^{2}}{2}}\mathrm{d}t
$$
E. $$
A_{1}\left(x\right)=1-\phi\left(\frac{x-2}{4}\right), \\A_{2}\left(x\right)=\phi\left(\frac{x-3}{4}\right)
\\A_{3}\left(x\right)=1-A_{1}\left(x\right)-A_{2}\left(x\right)
\\\phi(x)=\frac{1}{2\pi}\int_{-\infty}^{x}\mathrm{e}^{-\frac{t^{2}}{2}}\mathrm{d}t
$$
F. $$
A_{1}\left(x\right)=\phi\left(\frac{x-1}{2}\right), \\A_{2}\left(x\right)=1-\phi\left(\frac{x-3}{2}\right)
\\A_{3}\left(x\right)=A_{1}\left(x\right)A_{2}\left(x\right)
\\\phi(x)=\frac{1}{2\pi}\int_{-\infty}^{x}\mathrm{e}^{-\frac{t}{2}}\mathrm{d}t
$$
G. $$
A_{1}\left(x\right)=1-\phi\left(\frac{x-1}{4}\right), \\A_{2}\left(x\right)=\phi\left(\frac{x-2}{4}\right)
\\A_{3}\left(x\right)=1-A_{1}\left(x\right)-A_{2}\left(x\right)
\\\phi(x)=\frac{1}{\sqrt{\pi}}\int_{-\infty}^{x}\mathrm{e}^{-\frac{t^{2}}{2}}\mathrm{d}t
$$
H. $$
A_{1}\left(x\right)=1-\phi\left(\frac{x-1}{2}\right), \\A_{2}\left(x\right)=1-\phi\left(\frac{x-2}{2}\right)
\\A_{3}\left(x\right)=A_{1}\left(x\right)A_{2}\left(x\right)
\\\phi(x)=\frac{1}{2\pi}\int_{-\infty}^{x}\mathrm{e}^{-\frac{t^{2}}{2}}\mathrm{d}t
$$
I. $$
A_{1}\left(x\right)=1-\phi\left(\frac{x-2}{2}\right), \\A_{2}\left(x\right)=\phi\left(\frac{x-3}{2}\right)
\\A_{3}\left(x\right)=1-A_{1}\left(x\right)-A_{2}\left(x\right)
\\\phi(x)=\frac{1}{\sqrt{\pi}}\int_{-\infty}^{x}\mathrm{e}^{-\frac{t}{2}}\mathrm{d}t
$$
J. $$
A_{1}\left(x\right)=\phi\left(\frac{x-1}{2}\right), \\A_{2}\left(x\right)=1-\phi\left(\frac{x-2}{2}\right)
\\A_{3}\left(x\right)=A_{1}\left(x\right)A_{2}\left(x\right)
\\\phi(x)=\frac{1}{2\pi}\int_{-\infty}^{x}\mathrm{e}^{-\frac{t^{2}}{4}}\mathrm{d}t
$$
